feedback.text: I want to join in suggesting inclusion of another practice: Publishers permanently disabling video games they have already sold. Many publishers such as Ubisoft include certain conditions in their TOS which allows them to permanently shut down their games for any reason. This is shady and I'm assuming most gamers don't know about this, this is unreasonable seeing as any event like for example, the publisher going bankrupt would strip gamers of the games they have purchased. Personally I would think that when I have purchased a game, I own it, but these publishers are drifting in a direction which would make buying games similar to buying access to a temporary service that they can shut down without warning. I think it would be fantastic if you included this as a practice listed in the Digital Fairness Act.
